questionnaires.title: AI System Compliance Check
questionnaires.description: Help us determine if your solution needs to comply with the EU AI Act

sections.title: AI System & Model Identification
sections.description: Let's identify if your solution is an AI system, an AI model, or something else

questions.question: Is your solution built using machines or computers?
questions.help_text: Does it run on hardware like servers, computers, or embedded systems?

no.action: Skip to General-Purpose AI Model questions

questions.question: Can your solution work independently without constant human control?
questions.help_text: Can it make decisions or take actions on its own, at least to some degree?

no.action: Your solution may not be an AI system. Check if it's a General-Purpose AI Model

questions.question: Can your solution learn or improve after it's been deployed?
questions.help_text: This is optional - it's okay if your system doesn't self-learn

no.action: That's fine. Continue to next question

optional.action: This feature is optional. Continue to next question

questions.question: Does your solution have specific goals it's trying to achieve?
questions.help_text: These goals might be explicitly programmed or learned from data

no.action: Your solution may not be an AI system. Check if it's a General-Purpose AI Model

questions.question: Does your solution analyze input data to create outputs?
questions.help_text: For example: predictions, recommendations, decisions, or generated content

no.action: Your solution may not be an AI system. Check if it's a General-Purpose AI Model

questions.question: What type of outputs does your solution produce?
questions.help_text: Select all outputs that your system can generate during operation

questions.question: Can your solution's outputs affect physical or digital systems?
questions.help_text: For example: controlling equipment, managing energy flows, or updating digital systems

yes.action: Your solution appears to be an AI system. See results

no.action: Your solution may not be an AI system. Check if it's a General-Purpose AI Model

questions.question: Assessment Result: AI System Classification
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your solution is likely an AI System under the EU AI Act.

continue.action: Continue to Section 2: Determine Your Role

questions.notes: Traditional rule-based software (where all rules are manually programmed by humans) is NOT considered an AI system

questions.question: Is your solution a general-purpose AI model (like ChatGPT or similar)?
questions.help_text: General-purpose AI models can perform many different tasks and are often used as building blocks in other applications

yes.action: Continue to verify it's a general-purpose AI model

no.action: Your solution may not fall under the AI Act

questions.question: Was your model trained on large amounts of data?
questions.help_text: Did it learn from extensive datasets without requiring labeled examples for every task?

no.action: Your solution may not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Can your model perform many different types of tasks?
questions.help_text: Is it versatile enough to handle various problems, not just one specific function?

no.action: Your solution may not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Can your model be integrated into different applications or systems?
questions.help_text: Can other developers or systems use your model as a component?

no.action: Your solution may not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Has your model been released on the market or is it used beyond research/testing?
questions.help_text: Is it available for commercial or operational use, not just for internal research?

no.action: Your model may be exempt as it's still in research/testing phase

questions.question: How much computing power was used to train your model?
questions.help_text: This helps determine if it's a foundation model with significant capabilities

options.action: Continue to next question - potential high-impact model
Continue to next question - you may need to determine this later

questions.question: What can your model generate or produce?
questions.help_text: Select all types of output that your model is capable of generating

none_checked.action: No generative capabilities - reviewing other criteria

questions.question: Assessment Result: General-Purpose AI Model
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your solution is likely a General-Purpose AI Model under the EU AI Act.

switch_questionnaire.action: Switch to AI Model Questionnaire for specific obligations

continue_both.action: If your model is integrated into a system, continue to assess the complete AI system as well

questions.notes: AI models are components of AI systems but are not complete systems on their own. If your model is part of a larger system, you may need to complete both questionnaires.

questions.question: Assessment Result: Outside AI Act Scope
questions.result_text: Your solution likely falls outside the scope of the EU AI Act

finish.action: Assessment complete - no AI Act obligations apply

reassess.action: Start over if you want to reassess

questions.notes: This does not constitute legal advice. If your solution changes significantly, you should reassess.

sections.title: Operator Role & Responsibility Definition
sections.description: Understanding your role helps determine which obligations apply to you

questions.question: Let's determine your role in relation to this AI system
questions.help_text: You may have multiple roles. We'll check each one.

questions.question: Did you develop or commission the development of this AI system?
questions.help_text: You're a provider if you created it or had someone create it for you

no.action: Check if you're a deployer instead

questions.question: Are you making this AI system available in the European Union market for the first time?
questions.help_text: This means being the first to offer it for distribution or use in the EU, regardless of whether it's free or paid
examples: Making an AI system available via API in the EU
Selling or distributing software with AI in the EU
Offering AI-powered services to EU customers

yes.action: You are a Provider - specific obligations apply

questions.question: Are you using the AI system under your own name or brand?
questions.help_text: Does it appear to customers/users as your product or service?

yes.action: You are a Provider - specific obligations apply

no.action: Check if you're a deployer instead

questions.question: Role Assessment Result: You are a PROVIDER
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, you are a Provider of this AI system. Providers have significant obligations under the AI Act.

continue.action: Check if you also modified someone else's system (making you provider of that too)

skip_to_exemptions.action: Skip to check if exemptions apply

questions.question: Are you using an AI system developed by someone else?
questions.help_text: You're a deployer if you're operating or using an AI system in your organization

no.action: Check if you modified someone else's system

questions.question: Do you have authority and responsibility for how this AI system is used?
questions.help_text: For example: deciding when and how to use it, who uses it, and for what purposes
examples: A grid operator using an AI tool for optimization
A company using AI for predictive maintenance
A facility using AI for energy management

yes.action: You are a Deployer - specific obligations apply

no.action: Check if you modified someone else's system

questions.question: Role Assessment Result: You are a DEPLOYER
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, you are a Deployer of this AI system. Deployers have important obligations under the AI Act.

continue.action: Continue to check for other potential roles

skip_to_exemptions.action: Skip to check if exemptions apply

questions.question: Did you take someone else's high-risk AI system and do any of the following?
questions.help_text: Select any that apply

options.text: Put your own name or brand on it
Made substantial changes to how it works
Changed its intended purpose or use case

questions.question: Please describe the substantial changes you made:
questions.help_text: Substantial modifications are changes made after market release that weren't originally planned and affect compliance
examples: Modified the AI model or algorithms
Changed what the system is designed to do
Altered how the system makes decisions

submitted.action: Based on your modifications, you may now be considered a Provider

questions.question: Role Assessment Result: You are now a PROVIDER (through modification)
questions.result_text: Because you substantially modified or rebranded someone else's AI system, you are now considered the Provider of that system.

skip_to_exemptions.action: Skip to check if exemptions apply

questions.question: Is your AI system built into a physical product (like equipment or machinery)?
questions.help_text: For example: sensors, controllers, safety systems, or monitoring equipment

yes.action: Continue to assess product manufacturer status

questions.question: Is this AI system critical for the product's safety?
questions.help_text: Would the product be unsafe if the AI system failed or malfunctioned?

questions.question: Are you selling or deploying this product under your name or brand?

yes.action: You are also a Provider as product manufacturer

questions.question: Role Assessment Result: You are a PROVIDER (as product manufacturer)
questions.result_text: Because your AI system is a safety component of a product sold under your name, you are the Provider of the high-risk AI system.

questions.question: Your Role(s) Summary
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, we have determined your specific standing under the AI Act framework.

continue_provider.action: You have been identified as a Provider. Continue to Section 3 to check for specific exemptions.

continue_deployer.action: You have been identified as a Deployer. Continue to Section 3 to check for specific exemptions.

no_role.action: No specific AI Act role (Provider or Deployer) was identified based on your responses. You may not have direct obligations at this stage.

questions.notes: If you have multiple roles (e.g., both Provider and Deployer), you must comply with all applicable obligations for each role.

sections.title: Scope & Exemptions Analysis
sections.description: Some AI systems are exempt from certain requirements

questions.question: Let's check if any exemptions apply to your AI system
Is your AI system used exclusively for military, defense, or national security purposes?
questions.help_text: It must be developed AND used only for these purposes

yes.action: Your system is exempt from the AI Act

questions.question: Exemption Result: Military/Defense Exemption
questions.result_text: Your system is exempt from the AI Act due to military, defense, or national security use.

questions.question: Is your AI system used solely for scientific research and development?
questions.help_text: Not for commercial use or operational deployment

yes.action: Your system is exempt from the AI Act

questions.question: Exemption Result: Research Exemption
questions.result_text: Your system is exempt from the AI Act as it's used solely for scientific research.

finish.action: Assessment complete - but ethical standards still apply

questions.notes: Recognized ethical and professional standards for scientific research still apply

questions.question: Is your AI system still in testing or development and not yet released to market?
questions.help_text: Pre-market testing and prototyping are typically exempt

questions.question: Exemption Result: Pre-Market Testing Exemption
questions.result_text: Your system is currently exempt as it's in pre-market testing/development phase.

finish.action: Assessment complete - reassess before market release

questions.notes: You must reassess compliance before placing the system on the market

questions.question: Is your AI system available as free and open-source software?
questions.help_text: Users can freely run, copy, distribute, study, change, and improve it

no.action: No exemptions apply - continue to risk assessment

questions.question: Are all model parameters publicly available?
questions.help_text: Including weights, architecture information, and usage instructions

no.action: Doesn't qualify for open-source exemption - continue to risk assessment

questions.question: Is your system classified as high-risk OR does it fall under prohibited practices?
questions.help_text: We'll determine this in the next section if you're not sure

yes.action: Open-source systems still must comply if high-risk or prohibited

no.action: Some exemptions may apply to your open-source system

not_sure.action: Continue to risk assessment to determine this

questions.question: Exemption Result: Partial Open-Source Exemption
questions.result_text: As an open-source system that's not high-risk, certain obligations may not apply. However, you must still complete risk assessment.

questions.question: Exemption Result: No Exemptions Apply
questions.result_text: No exemptions apply to your AI system. You must complete the full compliance assessment.

sections.title: AI System Risk Classification
sections.description: The AI Act classifies systems by risk level: Unacceptable, High, Limited, or Minimal

questions.question: Let's determine your AI system's risk level
questions.help_text: This determines which obligations apply to you

questions.question: Does your AI system manipulate people's behavior in harmful ways?
questions.help_text: Using subliminal techniques or exploiting vulnerabilities to cause significant harm
examples: Misleading information that tricks people into bad decisions
Manipulating vulnerable groups (elderly, disabled, economically disadvantaged)

questions.question: Does it create 'social scoring' systems?
questions.help_text: Evaluating or classifying people based on their social behavior or personal characteristics in ways that harm them

questions.question: Does it predict criminal behavior based solely on profiling?
questions.help_text: Without objective evidence of actual involvement in criminal activity

questions.question: Does it create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping?
questions.help_text: Collecting facial images from the internet or CCTV without specific purpose

questions.question: Does it detect emotions in workplace or educational settings?
questions.help_text: Unless it's for medical or safety reasons

questions.question: Is the emotion detection for medical or safety reasons?
Does it categorize people based on sensitive characteristics?
questions.help_text: Such as race, political opinions, religion, sexual orientation, etc., inferred from biometric data

no.action: Your system is not prohibited - continue to high-risk assessment

questions.question: CRITICAL: Your AI System is PROHIBITED
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your AI system uses prohibited practices under the EU AI Act. You CANNOT deploy this system.

understand.action: Assessment complete - immediate review and system redesign required

review_answer.action: Go back to review your answers

questions.question: Result: Not Prohibited
questions.result_text: Your system doesn't fall under prohibited practices. Let's continue to check if it's high-risk.

questions.question: Is your AI system used for managing or operating critical infrastructure?
questions.help_text: This is the most relevant category for the energy sector
examples: Managing power grid supply and demand

questions.question: Is your system a safety component for this infrastructure?
questions.help_text: Is it used to directly protect physical infrastructure, health, or safety of people?

no.action: May not be high-risk - check other categories

questions.question: Could malfunction of your AI system lead to safety issues or service disruptions?
Does your system identify people remotely using biometric data?
questions.help_text: Such as facial recognition or other biometric identification

questions.question: Does your system categorize people based on biometric data?
questions.help_text: Assigning people to categories based on their biometric characteristics

questions.question: Does your system recognize emotions?
questions.help_text: Identifying or inferring emotions or intentions from biometric data (excluding prohibited workplace use)

questions.question: Is your system used to evaluate creditworthiness or create credit scores?
questions.help_text: Making financial decisions about individuals

questions.question: Is your system used for recruitment or employee selection?
Is your system used to make workplace decisions about employees?
questions.help_text: Such as promotions, terminations, task allocation, or performance monitoring

no.action: Your system is not high-risk under these categories

questions.question: Result: Your System May Be HIGH-RISK
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your system appears to be in a high-risk category. Let's check if any exceptions apply.

questions.question: Does your AI system only perform a narrow, specific task?
questions.help_text: Such as transforming data from one format to another

no.action: No exception applies - system is high-risk

questions.question: Is your system only used to improve results of previous human work?
questions.help_text: Not making the primary decision, just enhancing human decisions

no.action: No exception applies - system is high-risk

questions.question: Does your system only detect patterns or flag anomalies for human review?
questions.help_text: Humans make the final decisions, not the AI

no.action: No exception applies - system is high-risk

questions.question: Does your system perform only preparatory tasks?
questions.help_text: Gathering or organizing information for human assessment

no.action: No exception applies - system is high-risk

questions.question: Does your system pose no significant risk of harm to health, safety, or fundamental rights?

no.action: No exception applies - system is high-risk

questions.question: Does your system perform profiling of natural persons?
questions.help_text: Profiling means analyzing or predicting personal aspects

yes.action: Exception does NOT apply due to profiling - system is high-risk

no.action: Exception may apply - system might not be high-risk

questions.question: Result: High-Risk Exception May Apply
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your system might qualify for a high-risk exception. However, you must still document this assessment and register the system.

continue.action: Skip to Section 7: Transparency Requirements (Limited Risk)

questions.notes: You must document why your system is not high-risk and register this assessment.

questions.question: Result: Not High-Risk
questions.result_text: Your system doesn't fall under high-risk categories based on your answers.

questions.question: CONFIRMED: Your System is HIGH-RISK
questions.result_text: Your system is classified as HIGH-RISK under the EU AI Act. Significant compliance obligations apply.

both_roles.action: Continue to Section 5: Provider Obligations (you'll do Deployer after)

sections.title: Provider Obligations (High-Risk Systems)
sections.description: If you're a provider of a high-risk AI system, you must meet these requirements

questions.question: Provider Obligations Overview
questions.help_text: As a provider of a high-risk AI system, you have extensive obligations. Let's go through them step by step.

questions.question: Have you identified potential risks your AI system could create?
questions.help_text: For health, safety, or fundamental rights when used as intended

questions.question: Have you estimated and evaluated these risks?
Do you monitor your system after deployment to identify new risks?
questions.help_text: Using data from real-world use

questions.question: Have you implemented measures to address identified risks?
questions.help_text: To reduce risk to an acceptable level

questions.question: Do you test your AI system to ensure it performs as intended?
questions.help_text: With defined metrics and thresholds

yes.action: Risk Management complete - Continue to Data Quality

questions.question: Do you have documented processes for data collection and management?
questions.help_text: Including where data comes from and how it's prepared

questions.question: Is your data relevant and representative for your AI system's intended use?
questions.help_text: Does it reflect real-world conditions where the system will operate?
examples: Grid measurements from actual power systems

questions.question: Have you checked your data for errors and ensured it's complete?
Have you examined your data for potential biases?
Have you implemented measures to detect and prevent bias?
Do you process sensitive personal data (race, health, etc.)?
questions.help_text: Only if strictly necessary for bias detection

no.action: Data Quality complete - Continue to Documentation

questions.question: Do you comply with GDPR and data protection regulations for this sensitive data?

yes.action: Data Quality complete - Continue to Documentation

no.action: ️ GDPR compliance required - Continue to Documentation

questions.question: Have you documented your AI system's design and development?
Have you documented the training process and data used?
Have you documented testing procedures and results?
Is this documentation up-to-date and available for authorities?
Does your system automatically record events relevant to safety and performance?
questions.help_text: This should be built into the system by default

questions.question: Do these logs help identify potential risks or issues?
Can deployers monitor system operation using these logs?
Can deployers understand how your system works?
questions.help_text: Is it designed to be sufficiently transparent?

questions.question: Have you provided instructions for use in an appropriate format?
questions.help_text: Complete, correct, and understandable

questions.question: Do your instructions include all required information?
questions.help_text: Check all that you've included

all_checked.action: Transparency complete - Continue to Human Oversight

some_checked.action: ️ All items required - Continue to Human Oversight

none_checked.action: ️ All items required - Continue to Human Oversight

questions.question: Is your system designed to allow human oversight during operation?
questions.help_text: Can humans monitor and intervene when needed?
examples: A person can intervene if AI proposes shutting down a power line
System provides alerts for human review

questions.question: Have you identified appropriate oversight measures?
questions.help_text: Either built into the system or for deployers to implement

questions.question: Do your oversight measures enable humans to:
questions.help_text: Check all that apply
options: Understand the system's capabilities and limitations
Decide not to use the system or ignore its recommendations
Intervene or interrupt system operation when needed

all_checked.action: Human Oversight complete - Continue to Technical Requirements

some_checked.action: ️ All capabilities required - Continue to Technical Requirements

none_checked.action: ️ All capabilities required - Continue to Technical Requirements

questions.question: Is your system accurate for its intended purpose?
questions.help_text: Can it reliably perform the tasks it was designed for?

questions.question: Have you documented accuracy levels and metrics?
Is your system resilient to errors and unexpected situations?
questions.help_text: Can it handle faults, inconsistencies, or unusual conditions safely?

questions.question: Does your system have protections against unauthorized modifications?
questions.help_text: Can it resist attempts by third parties to alter its operation?

questions.question: Have you implemented cybersecurity measures?
questions.help_text: Protection against attacks like data poisoning, adversarial examples, or model theft

yes.action: Technical Requirements complete - Continue to Provider Identification

questions.question: Are your name, brand, and contact details visible on the system or documentation?

yes.action: Provider ID complete - Continue to Quality Management

questions.question: Have you documented policies and procedures for regulatory compliance?
Do you have systematic processes for design and development?
Do you have quality control and assurance procedures?
Do you have testing and validation procedures?
Do you have a post-market monitoring system?
questions.help_text: To collect and analyze data about system performance after deployment

questions.question: Do you have procedures for reporting serious incidents?

yes.action: Quality Management complete - Continue to Document Retention

questions.question: Will you retain all required documentation for 10 years after market release?
questions.help_text: Including technical docs, quality management docs, and compliance certificates

yes.action: Document Retention complete - Continue to Conformity Assessment

questions.question: Which conformity assessment procedure applies to your system?

options.text: Internal control (self-assessment) - for most critical infrastructure systems
Assessment with notified body - required when harmonized standards don't exist or weren't fully applied
Product-specific assessment - for AI systems built into regulated products

options.action: ️ You need to determine this - Continue

questions.question: Have you completed the conformity assessment?

yes.action: Conformity Assessment complete - Continue to Declaration

no.action: ️ Required before market release - Continue to Declaration

questions.question: Have you signed a declaration stating your system meets all requirements?
Does your declaration include all required information?
questions.help_text: System identification, your details, conformity statement, standards applied, date and signature

yes.action: Declaration complete - Continue to CE Marking

no.action: ️ All information required - Continue to CE Marking

questions.question: Is the CE marking visible on your system's packaging or documentation?

yes.action: Continue to verify CE marking quality

questions.question: Is the CE marking legible and permanent?

yes.action: CE Marking complete - Continue to Registration

no.action: ️ Must be legible and permanent - Continue to Registration

questions.question: Where should your system be registered?

options.text: National level (for critical infrastructure in energy sector)

options.action: ️ You need to determine this - Continue

questions.question: Have you completed the registration?

yes.action: Registration complete - Continue to Corrective Actions

questions.question: If you discover your system doesn't comply, will you take corrective action?
Will you investigate the causes of non-compliance?
Will you inform authorities if your system presents risks?

yes.action: Corrective Actions complete - See Provider Obligations Summary

no.action: ️ Required by law - See Provider Obligations Summary

questions.question: Provider Obligations Assessment Complete
questions.result_text: You've completed the Provider obligations assessment. Review your compliance status below.

continue_deployer.action: If you're also a Deployer, continue to Section 6

sections.title: Deployer Obligations (High-Risk Systems)
sections.description: If you're using (deploying) a high-risk AI system, you must meet these requirements

questions.question: Deployer Obligations Overview
questions.help_text: As a deployer of a high-risk AI system, you have important obligations. Let's review them.

questions.question: Have you implemented measures to ensure you follow the system's instructions for use?

yes.action: Instructions compliance - Continue to Human Oversight

questions.question: Have you assigned people who understand the technology and domain?
questions.help_text: They need both technical knowledge and expertise in the application area

questions.question: Have these people been trained?
Do they have authority to override or stop the system?
Do they have necessary support and resources?

yes.action: Human Oversight complete - Continue to Input Data

questions.question: Do you control the input data for this system?

no.action: Input data quality not applicable - Continue to Monitoring

questions.question: Is your input data relevant for the system's intended purpose?
Is your input data representative of real conditions?

yes.action: Input Data complete - Continue to Monitoring

questions.question: Do you monitor how the system performs based on instructions?
Do you have mechanisms to report information to the provider?
questions.help_text: For their post-market monitoring

questions.question: Would you inform the provider and authorities if the system presents risks?
Would you report serious incidents to the provider and authorities?

yes.action: Monitoring complete - Continue to Log Retention

questions.question: Do you keep automatically generated logs for at least 6 months?
questions.help_text: Only if the logs are under your control

yes.action: Log Retention complete - Continue to Worker Information

no.action: ️ Required if logs are under your control - Continue to Worker Information

questions.question: Does your system affect workers or employees?

no.action: Worker information not applicable - Continue to Registration

questions.question: Have you informed worker representatives and affected workers?
questions.help_text: That they'll be subject to the AI system's use

yes.action: Worker Information complete - Continue to Registration

questions.question: Are you a public authority or institution?

no.action: Registration not applicable - Continue to DPIA

questions.question: Have you registered yourself and the system before using it?
questions.help_text: In the EU database (except for critical infrastructure systems)

no.action: ️ Required for public authorities - Continue to DPIA

questions.question: Is a Data Protection Impact Assessment required for your use case?
questions.help_text: Check with your Data Protection Officer or authority

no.action: DPIA not required - Continue to Information Provision

questions.question: Have you used the provider's instructions to help conduct the DPIA?

yes.action: DPIA complete - Continue to Information Provision

questions.question: Does your system make or assist in making decisions about people?
questions.help_text: Especially in critical infrastructure management

no.action: Information provision not applicable - Continue to FRIA

questions.question: Have you informed those people that they're subject to the AI system?

yes.action: Information Provision complete - Continue to FRIA

questions.question: Are you a public authority or entity providing public services?

yes.action: FRIA may be required - Continue to verify

no.action: Continue to check other FRIA requirements

questions.question: Does your system evaluate creditworthiness or credit scores?

no.action: FRIA not applicable - See Deployer Summary

questions.question: Have you conducted a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment?
questions.help_text: Before using the system

no.action: ️ Required for your type of deployer - Continue

questions.question: Have you notified the results to the market surveillance authority?
Deployer Obligations Assessment Complete
questions.result_text: You've completed the Deployer obligations assessment. Review your compliance status below.

sections.title: Transparency Requirements (Limited Risk)
sections.description: Even if your system isn't high-risk, transparency obligations may apply

questions.question: Transparency Obligations Overview
questions.help_text: Certain AI systems must meet transparency requirements regardless of their risk level.

questions.question: Does your AI system interact directly with people?
examples: Smart home systems with user interfaces

questions.question: Do people know they're interacting with an AI system?
questions.help_text: Is it clearly indicated, preferably at the first interaction?

yes.action: Interaction transparency complete - Continue to synthetic content

no.action: ️ Required - must inform users they're interacting with AI - Continue

questions.question: Does your system generate synthetic content?
questions.help_text: Such as text, images, audio, or video

questions.question: Is this content marked as AI-generated?
questions.help_text: In a way that's machine-readable and detectable

yes.action: Content marking complete - Continue to emotion/biometric

no.action: ️ Required - content must be marked as AI-generated - Continue

questions.question: Does your system recognize emotions?
Does it categorize people based on biometric data?
Have you informed people who are exposed to this system?

no.action: ️ Required - must inform exposed persons - Continue

questions.question: Do you process this data in compliance with GDPR?

yes.action: Emotion/biometric transparency complete - Continue to deepfake

no.action: ️ GDPR compliance required - Continue to deepfake

questions.question: Does your system create or manipulate deepfake content?
questions.help_text: Highly realistic content that could be mistaken for real

no.action: Continue to public information text check

questions.question: Does it generate text for public information purposes?
questions.help_text: Content published to inform the public on matters of public interest

no.action: No deepfake/public text disclosure needed - See Transparency Summary

questions.question: Do you disclose that this content was artificially generated?

yes.action: Deepfake disclosure complete - See Transparency Summary

no.action: ️ Required - must disclose AI-generated content - See Summary

questions.question: Transparency Requirements Assessment Complete
questions.result_text: You've completed the transparency obligations assessment.

continue_literacy.action: Continue to Section 8: AI Literacy (applies to all)

sections.title: AI Literacy (All Systems)
sections.description: This applies to everyone working with AI systems

questions.question: AI Literacy Requirements Overview
questions.help_text: All providers and deployers must ensure adequate AI literacy among their staff.

questions.question: Have you ensured your staff has adequate AI literacy?
questions.help_text: Do they understand how AI works, its benefits, and its limitations?

no.action: ️ Required for all AI systems - Continue

questions.question: Does your AI literacy program cover:
questions.help_text: Check all that apply
options: Understanding of AI within the organization
Clear definition of your organization's role (provider/deployer)
Awareness of risks posed by AI systems
Technical knowledge appropriate to staff roles
Training tailored to different user groups (technical vs. non-technical)

all_checked.action: AI Literacy complete - See Final Summary

some_checked.action: ️ All elements recommended - See Final Summary

none_checked.action: ️ AI literacy program needed - See Final Summary

questions.question: AI Literacy Assessment Complete
questions.result_text: You've completed all sections of the AI System questionnaire.

restart.action: Start over with a new assessment

questionnaires.title: General-Purpose AI Model Compliance Check
questionnaires.description: For general-purpose AI models under the EU AI Act

sections.title: General-Purpose AI Model Identification
sections.description: Let's verify if your model qualifies under the AI Act

questions.question: General-Purpose AI Model Assessment
questions.help_text: This questionnaire is for AI models that can perform many different tasks. If your model is part of a specific AI system, you may also need to complete the AI System questionnaire.

questions.question: Is your model trained with large amounts of data using self-supervised learning?
questions.help_text: Self-supervised means it learned patterns from data without needing labels for every example

no.action: May not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Can your model perform many different types of tasks?
questions.help_text: Is it versatile enough to handle various problems, not just one specific function?

no.action: May not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Can your model be integrated into different applications or systems?
questions.help_text: Can other developers or systems use your model as a component?

no.action: May not be a general-purpose AI model

questions.question: Is your model currently used for research, development, or prototyping only?
questions.help_text: Or has it been placed on the market/made available?

no.action: Continue - your model appears to be in scope

questions.question: How much computing power was used to train your model?
questions.help_text: This helps determine if it has systemic risk

options.text: More than 10^25 FLOP (very high - potential systemic risk)
options.action: Your model has SYSTEMIC RISK - additional obligations apply
️ You'll need to determine this - Continue

questions.question: What can your model generate or produce?
questions.help_text: Select all that apply to your model's capabilities

questions.question: Assessment Result: Not a General-Purpose AI Model
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, your model doesn't appear to be a general-purpose AI model under the AI Act.

check_ai_system.action: Check if it's an AI System instead

questions.question: Assessment Result: Research Exemption
questions.result_text: Your model is exempt from the AI Act as it's used solely for research, development, or prototyping.

finish.action: Assessment complete - reassess before market release

questions.notes: You must reassess before placing the model on the market

questions.question: Confirmed: General-Purpose AI Model
questions.result_text: Your model is a General-Purpose AI Model under the EU AI Act.

continue.action: Continue to determine your role and obligations

questions.question: IMPORTANT: Systemic Risk Model
questions.result_text: Your model is classified as having SYSTEMIC RISK due to its high computing power (>10^25 FLOP). Additional strict obligations apply.

continue.action: Continue to provider obligations (including systemic risk requirements)

sections.title: Role Definition: AI Provider
sections.description: Determine if you're the provider of this model

questions.question: Let's determine if you're a provider of this general-purpose AI model
Did you develop this AI model or commission its development?

no.action: You may not be a provider

questions.question: Are you making this model available on the EU market?
questions.help_text: Or are you established in the EU and the model's output is used in the EU?

yes.action: You are a Provider - obligations apply

no.action: You may not fall under EU AI Act jurisdiction

questions.question: Role Confirmed: You are a PROVIDER
questions.result_text: As a provider of a general-purpose AI model, specific obligations apply to you.

questions.question: Assessment Result: Not a Provider
questions.result_text: Based on your answers, you don't appear to be a provider under the EU AI Act.

finish.action: Assessment complete - no AI Act obligations

sections.title: Applicability of Exemptions
sections.description: Check if your model qualifies for exemptions

questions.question: Let's check if any exemptions apply to your model
Is your model released under a free and open-source license?
questions.help_text: Users can freely use, modify, and distribute it

questions.question: Are all model parameters publicly available?
questions.help_text: Including weights, architecture, and usage information

no.action: Doesn't qualify for open-source exemption - check other exemptions

questions.question: Does your model have systemic risk (>10^25 FLOP training compute)?

yes.action: Open-source exemption does NOT apply to systemic risk models

questions.question: Is your model used solely for scientific research and development?
questions.help_text: Not for commercial purposes or operational use

questions.question: Is your model still in testing or development and not yet released?
Result: Partial Open-Source Exemption
questions.result_text: Some obligations may not apply to your open-source model. However, you still have certain transparency obligations.

questions.question: Result: Research Exemption
questions.result_text: Your model is exempt as it's used solely for research.

finish.action: Assessment complete - ethical standards still apply

questions.question: Result: Pre-Market Exemption
questions.result_text: Your model is currently exempt as it's in pre-market testing.

finish.action: Assessment complete - reassess before market release

questions.question: Result: No Exemptions Apply
questions.result_text: You must comply with all provider obligations for general-purpose AI models.

sections.title: Provider Obligations for General-Purpose AI Models

questions.question: Provider Obligations Overview
questions.help_text: As a provider of a general-purpose AI model, you have specific obligations under the AI Act.

questions.question: Have you adhered to the Code of Practice for general-purpose AI models?
questions.help_text: The Code of Practice provides detailed guidance on meeting your obligations

yes.action: Code adherence simplifies compliance - Continue to summary requirements

no.action: You must demonstrate alternative compliance measures

questions.question: Why are you not adhering to the Code of Practice?
questions.help_text: This is for documentation purposes

submitted.action: You must meet obligations through alternative adequate means

questions.question: Have you created and maintained technical documentation of your model?
questions.help_text: Including training process, testing, and evaluation results

questions.question: Have you provided information and documentation to downstream providers?
questions.help_text: Providers who will integrate your model into their AI systems need specific information

questions.question: Have you implemented a policy to comply with EU copyright law?
questions.help_text: Including respecting text and data mining opt-outs

no.action: ️ Required - Continue to training data summary

questions.question: Have you published a summary of the content used for training your model?
questions.help_text: Using the template provided by the AI Office

questions.question: Is your organization established in a third country (outside the EU)?
Have you appointed an authorized representative in the EU?

yes.action: Representative appointed - Continue to systemic risk check

no.action: ️ Required for third-country providers - Continue to systemic risk check

questions.question: Does your model have systemic risk?
questions.help_text: Training compute >10^25 FLOP or designated by the Commission

yes.action: ️ Additional systemic risk obligations apply - Continue

questions.question: Have you performed model evaluation to assess systemic risks?
questions.help_text: Including adversarial testing and assessment of dangerous capabilities

no.action: ️ Required for systemic risk models - Continue

questions.question: Have you assessed and mitigated possible systemic risks?
questions.help_text: Including sources of risk and implemented mitigation measures

no.action: ️ Required for systemic risk models - Continue

questions.question: Do you track and report serious incidents to the AI Office?
questions.help_text: And report possible corrective measures

no.action: ️ Required for systemic risk models - Continue

questions.question: Have you ensured adequate cybersecurity protection for your model?
questions.help_text: Appropriate to the level of systemic risk

yes.action: Systemic risk obligations complete - See summary

no.action: ️ Required for systemic risk models - See summary

questions.question: Provider Obligations Assessment Complete
questions.result_text: You've completed all obligations for general-purpose AI model providers.

metadata.notes: This questionnaire structure provides explicit navigation for each answer. Each question specifies the next question ID based on the user's response. This does not constitute legal advice and should be used for informational purposes only.
